SUMMARY:
# Church Tech Trends: The Future of Digital Ministry in 2025 From technology in worship to online church engagement, how you connect with your congregation is evolving fast. Technology isn’t just changing the world; it’s transforming how churches worship, connect and operate. Interactive prayer, live Q&As and real-time chat features are shaping online church engagement, making digital worship feel just as meaningful as sitting in a pew. Digital ministry trends like virtual worship, mobile giving and church management software are transforming church operations. Imagine reaching more people, strengthening digital church engagement and making generosity effortless. Our church tech trends resource shows how digital ministry trends and online church technology drive growth and generosity.

# Church Tech Trends: The Future of Digital Ministry in 2025

## Share

![Illustration of various church tech tools](https://www.vancopayments.com/hs-fs/hubfs/Illustration%20of%20various%20church%20tech%20tools.png?width=1024&height=768&name=Illustration%20of%20various%20church%20tech%20tools.png)

![Illustration of various church tech tools](https://www.vancopayments.com/hs-fs/hubfs/Illustration%20of%20various%20church%20tech%20tools.png?width=1024&height=768&name=Illustration%20of%20various%20church%20tech%20tools.png)

Can you imagine running a church today without technology? It’s almost unthinkable. Since 2020, churches have embraced digital tools at a record pace. When COVID-19 hit, [96 percent of U.S.](https://www.barna.com/research/in-person-over-online-church/#:~:text=Following%20the%20onset%20of%20COVID,gathering%20place%20for%20their%20church) Protestant pastors started streaming services, proving that digital ministry isn’t just an option; it’s essential.

However, streaming was just the beginning. From technology in worship to online church engagement, how you connect with your congregation is evolving fast. What’s next?

This guide breaks down the innovative church tech trends shaping 2025. In this post, you’ll find the latest modern church tech trends, real-world insights and practical takeaways to help you navigate tech innovations in churches in 2025.

Table of Contents

## The Benefits of Embracing Church Tech

![Visual guide for church tech](https://www.vancopayments.com/hs-fs/hubfs/Visual%20guide%20for%20church%20tech.png?width=588&height=342&name=Visual%20guide%20for%20church%20tech.png)

![Visual guide for church tech](https://www.vancopayments.com/hs-fs/hubfs/Visual%20guide%20for%20church%20tech.png?width=588&height=342&name=Visual%20guide%20for%20church%20tech.png)

### Enhanced Worship Experiences

Technology isn’t just changing the world; it’s transforming how churches worship, connect and operate. If you’ve ever felt like keeping up with church tech trends is overwhelming, you’re not alone. But the thing is, when done right, technology doesn’t replace the heart of ministry; it amplifies it.

#### Live Streaming & Multimedia Presentations

Not everyone can attend church in person every Sunday, but that doesn’t mean they should miss out. Online [church technology](/egiving/blog/use-of-technology-in-churches) allows you to bring worship into homes, hospitals and anywhere else life takes your congregation. Live streaming keeps people connected even when they can’t be in the building.

Sermons are more engaging when people can see the message, not just hear it. Multimedia presentations with scripture visuals, worship lyrics and video clips make your services more dynamic. Want to take it further? You can integrate online giving directly into your live stream, so generosity becomes part of the worship experience. Vanco’s live streaming giving tool makes it simple. Learn more [here](/egiving/church-live-streaming).

#### Virtual Worship Tools and Digital Audio/Visuals

Nothing pulls people out of worship like lousy audio. You know the struggle if you've ever cringed at a crackling mic or a blurry video stream. Quality modern church tech, from professional-grade cameras to crystal-clear sound systems, helps keep the focus on worship, not technical distractions.

If you’re unsure where to start, our live stream setup guide breaks it down step by step. And if you’re considering a fully digital ministry, the resource below can help.

[![Want to Start or Improve Your Online Worship Services?](https://www.vancopayments.com/hs-fs/hubfs/Church-Live-Stream-Setup-Guide.jpg?width=588&height=201&name=Church-Live-Stream-Setup-Guide.jpg)](https://www.vancopayments.com/egiving/asset-how-to-start-an-online-church-ministry?utm_campaign=In%20Blog%20Ad%20CTA&utm_source=Organic&utm_medium=Blog%20Traffic&utm_content=Virtual%20Ministry%20Resource)

![Want to Start or Improve Your Online Worship Services?](https://www.vancopayments.com/hs-fs/hubfs/Church-Live-Stream-Setup-Guide.jpg?width=588&height=201&name=Church-Live-Stream-Setup-Guide.jpg)

### Improved Community Engagement

#### Social Media & Online Communities

People don’t just interact in church lobbies anymore; they connect online. Social media and digital platforms allow members to stay engaged throughout the week. Whether for prayer requests, sermon reflections or event updates, digital church engagement keeps your community active beyond Sunday morning.

Want to see how churches use online tools to build stronger communities? This church-giving data resource offers valuable insights.

[![See How You Compare: Benchmark Giving Data & Insights from 25,000 Churches!](https://www.vancopayments.com/hs-fs/hubfs/church-giving-data-resource_blog_cta.jpg?width=588&height=214&name=church-giving-data-resource_blog_cta.jpg)](https://www.vancopayments.com/as-vanco-faith-combined-asset-church-stats?utm_campaign=In%20Blog%20Ad%20CTA&utm_source=Organic&utm_medium=Blog%20Traffic&utm_content=Combined-Data-Resource)

![See How You Compare: Benchmark Giving Data & Insights from 25,000 Churches!](https://www.vancopayments.com/hs-fs/hubfs/church-giving-data-resource_blog_cta.jpg?width=588&height=214&name=church-giving-data-resource_blog_cta.jpg)

#### 

#### Digital Outreach and Online Giving Platforms

![](https://play.vidyard.com/bt4n6oGHV9Eqa9dusAwfYD.jpg)

Let’s be honest: fewer people carry cash or checks these days. If giving requires extra effort, some won’t do it. Tech innovations in churches make generosity easier. Platforms like [Vanco](/egiving) offer text giving, mobile apps and recurring donations, making it simple for people to support your mission on a continuing basis.

That’s key because a study of 25,000 churches found that recurring givers donate [twice as much as one-time givers](/egiving/asset-church-giving-statistics-tithing). If there’s an easier way to increase generosity without extra [fundraising](/egiving/blog/fundraising-ideas-for-churches), why not use it? For a deeper look, check out this churchgoer-giving study.

[![Get the Insider Secrets of 1,000 Churchgoers' Giving Habits](https://www.vancopayments.com/hs-fs/hubfs/Churchgoer-Study-Vol5_blog_cta.jpg?width=588&height=201&name=Churchgoer-Study-Vol5_blog_cta.jpg)](https://www.vancopayments.com/egiving/asset-the-definitive-churchgoer-giving-study?utm_campaign=In%20Blog%20Ad%20CTA&utm_source=Organic&utm_medium=Blog%20Traffic&utm_content=churchgoer-giving-study)

![Get the Insider Secrets of 1,000 Churchgoers' Giving Habits](https://www.vancopayments.com/hs-fs/hubfs/Churchgoer-Study-Vol5_blog_cta.jpg?width=588&height=201&name=Churchgoer-Study-Vol5_blog_cta.jpg)

### Operational Efficiency & Administration

#### Church Management Software

Churches run on more than just faith; they run on schedules, volunteers, budgets and records. The right church technology solutions simplify operations, freeing up time for ministry. Trusted platforms like [ACS Technologies](https://www.acstechnologies.com/?srsltid=AfmBOopEiwmZklffoAcob1a59jrt-Mw9tuFrzag-XYWvQcnl5spaDZ7n), [Planning Center](https://www.planningcenter.com/) and [Church Windows](https://churchwindows.com/) can help with everything from event planning to member tracking.

If you’re using (or considering) a ChMS, Vanco [integrates with 60+ systems](/egiving/integrations) to [streamline giving](/egiving/blog/recurring-church-giving) and financial tracking. Less admin work with these seamless integrations means your church has more time for ministry.

#### Data Analytics and Member Communication Tools

Guesswork isn’t a strategy. The best decisions come from accurate data and from understanding attendance trends, giving habits and engagement levels. With technology in worship, you can personalize outreach, strengthen relationships and make smarter ministry decisions.

The future of church tech isn’t about keeping up with trends. It’s about using the right tools to deepen relationships, reach more people and serve better. If that sounds like something your church wants, now’s the time to start.

## Key Church Tech Trends for 2025

### Virtual Worship & Hybrid Services

What if Sunday morning wasn’t the only time people engaged with your church? That’s precisely where modern church tech is taking ministry. Virtual worship and hybrid services are now essential, allowing members to join from home, on the road or worldwide whenever is most convenient for them.

Live streaming alone isn’t enough anymore. People expect more than just watching a service; they want to be part of it. Interactive prayer, live Q&As and real-time chat features are shaping online church engagement, making digital worship feel just as meaningful as sitting in a pew.

### Mobile Giving & Online Donations

Giving should never be complicated. Today’s church technology solutions make donating as simple as sending a text. [Text giving](/egiving/text-to-give-guide) lets members contribute in seconds, while [mobile church giving](/egiving/church-app) provides a fully customizable experience, such as:

People are far more likely to give when they don’t have to dig through their wallets for cash. Vanco’s platform integrates with over 60 church management systems, ensuring a smooth, stress-free giving experience.

![](https://play.vidyard.com/t64MVSp4iTm8uJZPGi4E67.jpg)

### Digital Church Management Tools

Running a church without digital tools? That’s like trying to keep attendance with a clipboard and a pen; it technically works, but why make life more complicated?

From scheduling volunteers to tracking tithes, online church technology helps churches stay organized and efficient. The best platforms, like Planning Center and ACS Technologies, integrate directly with giving and communication tools, eliminating administrative headaches.

### Social Media & Community Engagement Platforms

If your church isn’t active on social media, you’re missing out on one of the most significant tech solutions for churches. Your members are already scrolling; why not meet them there?

Whether through Instagram devotionals, Facebook prayer groups or TikTok sermon snippets (yes, that’s a thing now), churches are using digital platforms to build community beyond Sunday morning.

### Future Tech Innovations (e.g., AI, VR in Ministry)

Here’s a question: What if technology could handle your busy work, freeing you to focus on ministry? That’s the promise of tech innovations in churches, especially with AI. [AI for churches](/egiving/blog/ai-for-churches) makes sermon prep, email responses and engagement tracking more manageable.

We’ve even developed 50+ advanced AI prompts and bots specifically designed for pastors and church leaders. Curious? Check them out below.

[![Free AI Bots & Tools to Save Time!](https://www.vancopayments.com/hs-fs/hubfs/free-ai-bots-tools-for-pastors.png?width=588&height=336&name=free-ai-bots-tools-for-pastors.png)](https://www.vancopayments.com/egiving/blog/chat-gpt-promopts-for-church-pastors)

![Free AI Bots & Tools to Save Time!](https://www.vancopayments.com/hs-fs/hubfs/free-ai-bots-tools-for-pastors.png?width=588&height=336&name=free-ai-bots-tools-for-pastors.png)

## Best Practices for Implementing Church Tech

### Training and Support for Staff and Volunteers

Technology isn’t the problem; lack of training is. You can have the best church technology solutions, but if your team doesn’t know how to use them, they’re expensive trinkets/. Set up hands-on training, record simple how-to videos and offer ongoing support.

Make it easy for staff and volunteers to ask questions without feeling like they should already know the answers.

Assign a few tech-savvy team members as go-to resources. When everyone is confident using modern church tech, things run smoother, frustration stays low and you don’t have to troubleshoot during Sunday services.

### Balancing Tradition with Innovation

Every church has members who still prefer printed bulletins and others who expect service reminders via text. You don’t have to pick a side. Tech innovations in churches should support your mission, not change it.

Online options make it easier for people to engage, but that doesn’t mean face-to-face connections disappear. Keep what’s sacred, but don’t ignore what’s practical.

If digital tools help people stay connected, involved and generous, why wouldn’t you use them? Thoughtful adoption of digital church engagement ensures you meet people where they are without alienating anyone.

### Ensuring Digital Security and Privacy

Security isn't optional if your church collects donations, stores member data or live streams services; it’s a responsibility.

Cyber threats aren’t just for big corporations; churches are targets, too. Strong passwords, two-factor authentication and encrypted giving platforms protect your church and its members.

Partner with trusted digital ministry and giving providers who understand church-specific security needs. Don’t assume everyone knows what a phishing scam looks like; regular training matters.

A secure digital transformation in ministry builds trust, keeps your congregation’s information safe and lets you focus on ministry without unnecessary risks.

## Case Studies & Success Stories

### VR Church: Worship Without Walls

What if Sunday service didn’t require a commute, a dress code or a physical building? That’s precisely what Pastor D.J. Soto envisioned when he launched [VR Church](https://www.fox5ny.com/news/virtual-reality-church-worshipping-in-the-metaverse#:~:text=D,Virtual%20Reality%20Church%20in%202016) in 2016. Here, worship isn’t just streamed; it’s experienced. Members slip on VR headsets, become avatars and enter a fully immersive church community.

The metaverse serves as their sanctuary, where sermons unfold in interactive 3D landscapes, Bible studies feel more like journeys and baptisms happen in virtual waters.

During the pandemic, attendance skyrocketed as people sought deeper connections from home. For some, this wasn’t just an experiment in online church engagement; it was the most real church they had ever attended.

One member said, “I feel more seen here than I ever did in a pew.” Of course, Pastor Soto doesn’t see this as a total replacement for in-person gatherings, but rather as a bold new chapter in technology in worship.

### AI-Generated Worship: Soulful or Soulless?

Could a robot preach a sermon that stirs your spirit? [Violet Crown City Church](https://www.christianpost.com/news/ai-generated-church-service-lacked-spiritual-depth-pastor-says.html#:~:text=It%20may%20not%20be%20the,generated%20Sunday%20service%20last%20month) in Austin decided to find out. Their pastor used ChatGPT to generate an entire Sunday service, prayers, sermon and liturgy to test the limits of digital ministry trends.

The verdict? Let’s say the congregation won’t invite AI back to the pulpit soon. While the service ran smoothly, it lacked something crucial: depth. Worshipers described it as flat and mechanical, like a well-crafted essay that forgot it was supposed to move hearts. The pastor said, “I’m glad we did it, but let’s never do it again.”

#### Bottom line

These stories prove that tech innovations in churches can create profound experiences or remind us why the human touch still matters. Either way, the digital future of ministry is already here. The question is: How will you use it?

## Challenges and How to Overcome Them

### Budget and Resource Constraints

You know technology could take your ministry to the next level, but the budget says otherwise. Does that sound familiar? Many churches hesitate to invest in church technology solutions because every dollar already has a mission.

However, here’s the reality: avoiding tech can cost you more in the long run. Outdated systems slow you down and limited digital giving options mean lost generosity. Instead of seeing it as an expense, consider it an investment in sustainability.

### Resistance to Change

You introduce a new tool and suddenly, it feels like you’ve suggested replacing the pews with bean bags. Change isn’t easy, especially in churches with deep-rooted traditions.

Still, digital transformation in ministry doesn’t erase what’s sacred; it strengthens it. The key is storytelling. Show how technology in worship creates deeper connections, not distractions. Once people see the impact, resistance fades.

### Technical Difficulties and Digital Literacy

Not everyone is born knowing how to navigate online church engagement tools. Yet frustration often comes from fear, not ability. Clear, step-by-step guidance makes a difference.

Offer hands-on training and, most importantly, patience. Adopting tech innovations in churches is a process, not a sprint.

## Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

### What are the most impactful church tech trends for 2025?

Digital ministry trends like virtual worship, mobile giving and church management software are transforming church operations.

### How can technology improve worship experiences?

Enabling live streaming, enhancing multimedia presentations and creating interactive worship sessions.

### What challenges do churches face in adopting new technology?

Common challenges include budget limitations, resistance from traditional members and the need for staff training.

### How do I get started with digital transformation in my church?

Begin by assessing your church’s needs, exploring affordable tech solutions and investing in training for your team.

## Final Thoughts

In 2025, church tech isn’t optional; it’s the bridge between tradition and the future. Imagine reaching more people, strengthening digital church engagement and making generosity effortless. Why struggle with outdated systems when modern church tech helps you focus on ministry instead of management?

The world is changing and so are the ways people worship. Are you ready? Our church tech trends resource shows how digital ministry trends and online church technology drive growth and generosity. Don’t just adapt; lead the way. Download it now!

## 9 digital church trends every ministry should know

Nowadays, hymn books make way for lyric projections, and sermons come alive on screensâthe fusion of faith and church technology is unmissable. Connectivity extends beyond physical spaces, and churches are embracing digital pathways to connect, inspire, and uplift their congregations.Â

No longer limited by the four walls of the sanctuary, faith now resonates in pixels and bytes, bridging the gap between tradition and tomorrow. These church trends mark a transformative shift defining the contours of contemporary worship.Â

## 1. How church apps are streamlining worship

Once considered a luxury or a tech-forward anomaly, church apps have seamlessly found their place in the palms of believers around the world.Â

Their explosive growth can be attributed to the modern congregation's demand for instant, on-the-go access to spiritual content, community updates, and outreach opportunities.

The debate around the necessity of apps for churches is heating up. Some traditionalists argue that the essence of worship may get diluted with too much technology. While an app might not be for everyone, we can think of a few [reasons why your church would benefit from an app](https://www.playlister.app/blog/4-reasons-your-ministry-doesnt-need-a-church-app-and-3-you-might). These tools are essential connectors in a world where digital natives are becoming the norm. A church app, when executed right, can be an extension of the ministry itself, encapsulating its mission, values, and community spirit in a touch-friendly interface.

While hymns and sermons will always remain central, the ways we access and interact with them are undeniably shifting. Church apps are here to stay, fortifying the bridge between age-old traditions and the digital future. If you're on board with these new church trends and want to learn more, here are the [top Christian apps](https://www.playlister.app/blog/the-top-21-christian-apps-you-didnt-know-you-needed) you can download today.

â

## 2. The digitization of donations and online church giving

Have you noticed that the traditional donation basket passed among pews is becoming more and more obsolete? It's giving way to a more efficient, contemporary counterpart: online church giving, a testament to how churches are adapting to the times. Recently, digital donations have seen a significant surge, reflecting the congregation's evolving preferences and the sheer convenience of e-giving.

There are undeniable advantages to integrating a church giving app into regular church operations.Â

For one, it opens the doors to consistent and uninterrupted donations. Regardless of physical attendance, members can contribute at any time, from anywhere. This also means the church has a steadier, more predictable stream of resources to support its activities and initiatives.

Plus, church platforms often come equipped with analytic tools, offering insights into giving patterns and helping churches understand and engage their community better. Itâs also environmentally friendlier, reducing the need for paper checks and envelopes.

For the modern believer, the act of giving is now just a few clicks away, merging the age-old virtue of generosity with the ease of digital technology. As churches continue to adapt, the blend of spirituality and innovation promises a brighter, more connected future for congregations worldwide.

â

## 3. Appealing announcements with digital signs for churches

Instantaneous communication is essential, ensuring no one misses an announcement or gets left behind. Now, churches are pivoting from traditional modes of conveying their messages. With hymn boards and printed bulletins slowly fading into the annals of church history, digital signs are rising as the contemporary communicators within sanctuaries.

This transition to digital signs is not merely a nod to technologyâit's a reflection of the modern worship environment's needs.Â

Digital church signs are versatile, allowing for real-time updates, fostering an engaging atmosphere, and catering to an audience accustomed to digital interfaces. They capture attention, delivering messages with both grace and gravitas, seamlessly integrating into the worship experience.

Beyond their primary communication function, these signs enhance a church's aesthetic, merging the traditional with the contemporary. They paint a welcoming picture, appealing to both long-time members and newcomers, making churches more accessible and relatable.Â

For those curious about how these digital marvels are revolutionizing church communication, here are [five ways you can use digital signage](https://www.playlister.app/blog/top-5-digital-signage-uses-for-your-church) at church. From event announcements to spotlighting community engagements, embrace digital signage and lead your congregation into an enriched, modern worship experience.

â

## 4. Live-streamed services on Sundays and beyond

Accessibility is everything, and while the tradition of Sunday services remains sacred, 2023 has seen a surge in live-streaming beyond this traditional time slot. Catering to a global audience and those unable to attend in person expands and maintains community across borders.

The appeal of live streaming is evident: it provides an expansive reach and fosters a sense of community even in virtual spaces. Nowadays, instead of relying solely on third-party platforms, churches are increasingly hosting live streams on their official websites. This shift bypasses the unpredictability of social media algorithms while fortifying the church's digital domain.Â

A testament to the growing emphasis on self-reliance and building up owned digital assets, live-streamed services boost online interactions. Immediacy and accessibility reign supreme, and live streams are becoming the new norm to cater to this, solidifying the church's position online while honoring its time-tested traditions.

â

## 5. Church management software and simple leadership

While today's sanctuaries gleam with the soft glow of state-of-the-art electronics designed to elevate the worship experience, the core of worship remains timelessly intact. Advanced audio-visual setups, interactive touchpoints, and enhanced communication tools are but a few of the wonders that modern church electronics bring to the pews, enacting the digital renaissance.

Shifting online can create more jobs for church leaders, which is why we have seen a distinct rise in the use of [church management software](https://www.playlister.app/blog/best-church-management-software-in-2022). These platforms transform administrative and pastoral tasks, offering a centralized hub for scheduling, community engagement, resource allocation, and much more. Leaders can now focus more on shepherding their flock spiritually, with the mundane yet essential tasks streamlined by these software solutions.

Churches are not just places of prayer anymore; they're hubs of activity, interaction, and community building. To manage these multifaceted operations, software tailored for churches has become indispensable. As the lines between the physical and digital church experience blur, these platforms stand as silent revolutionaries, ensuring that the church remains in step with the times without compromising its foundational ethos.

â

## 6. Digital marketing for churches

Attention is currency, and churches, like every other institution, must adapt to remain visible and relevant. Digital marketing has transcended the realm of businesses and now plays a pivotal role in expanding a church's mission and message.

Social media platforms, once mere networking tools, have evolved into powerful channels for faith communities. They offer unparalleled opportunities for churches to engage with their congregation, attract newcomers, and foster a sense of unity, even when miles apart.Â

Effective church digital marketing strategies might encompass:

Digital marketing for churches doesn't commercialize your ministry. Instead, it amplifies your purpose, ensuring the timeless message of faith finds its voice.

â

## 7. Data-driven decisions are a no-brainer

Just as gardeners prune to invigorate growth, churches today wield data as their tool to refine and rejuvenate their ministries. Gathering and using data about demographics, engagement levels, and financial records equips churches to make decisions that are both informed and impactful.

Taking a peek behind the numbers helps churches understand the heartbeat of their congregation, recognize trends, and anticipate needs. Such data-driven insights can highlight courses or programs that might be waning in relevance, allowing churches to refocus resources where they're most needed.

Recent shifts towards integrated church tech systems further underline this trend. Consolidated databases pave the way for comprehensive dashboards, providing leaders with a holistic view of their churchâs operations. Current church trends suggest that decision-making is no longer based on instinct but backed by insights.

â

## 8. Cybersecurity measuresâa new must-have

As congregations increasingly find solace online and church databases brim with personal information, cybersecurity is a moral imperative. The need for enhanced security measures has never been more pronounced. While once it may have seemed unimaginable that places of worship would fall prey to cyber-attacks, 2023 is a stark reminder that sacred spaces are not exempt from the pervasive threat of digital malevolence.

Churches, like any other institutions, manage vast swathes of sensitive data, from financial donations to personal contacts and even familial records. Unfortunately, this makes them attractive targets for cybercriminals aiming to exploit vulnerabilities for monetary or malicious intent.

As ransomware attacks grow in frequency and sophistication, churches must bolster their defenses. This goes beyond merely implementing firewalls or encrypted databases. It requires a culture of cybersecurity awareness, where staff and members are educated on the telltale signs of phishing attempts or potential breaches.

Moreover, churches must also liaise with cybersecurity professionals to conduct regular risk assessments, ensuring that their digital sanctuaries remain impregnable fortresses against cyber threats. As churches deepen their embrace of technology, they must also champion the cause of digital vigilance, preserving the sanctity and trust of their communities in an increasingly interconnected world.

â

## 9. Using church presentation software to revamp worshipÂ

Gone are the days when church services relied solely on hymnals and printed sermons. Today, the vibrant blend of tradition and technology is transforming the way we worship, with church presentation software at the forefront of this change.

Church presentation software has shifted from a novel addition to an integral component of interactive worship. Now, hymns are visually accompanied by breathtaking scenic visuals, scriptures seamlessly transition on large screens, and religious stories come alive through compelling animations. This is the potential of today's advanced software.

Traditional church presentations, while rich in heritage, were limited by static visuals and the constraints of physical mediums. Modern presentation tools, on the other hand, allow for real-time edits, multimedia integration, and even audience interaction, making the worship experience more immersive and adaptable.

Church presentation software solutions like [Playlister](https://www.playlister.app/) cater to a digitally savvy audience, ensuring that the age-old messages of faith are delivered in a way that speaks to people of the 21st century. They bridge the generation gap, appealing to older members who appreciate the core message and younger attendees who resonate with dynamic multimedia.

â

## Embracing the digital reformation as we chart the course ahead

As we stand on the precipice of change, it's undeniable that technology has become an integral part of our worship. From church apps that place spirituality at our fingertips to digital giving platforms that redefine generosity for the modern believer, the church has embarked on a transformative journey. It's a journey that blends tradition with innovation, ensuring faith remains evergreen.

While hymn books and traditional sermons have their place, the digital shiftâmarked by church presentation software and advanced analyticsâoffers an inclusive, interactive worship experience, appealing to both the traditionalist and the digital native. With the rise of cybersecurity threats, the church also realizes the importance of guarding its digital sanctuary, emphasizing the responsibility that comes with digital progression.

As we've navigated the church trends shaping churches in 2023, one thing becomes clear: while methods may evolve, the mission remains unchanged. The essence of the church, rooted in community, faith, and connection, continues to thrive, even as it dons a digital cloak.

Churches may adapt and evolve, but their core purposeâto cultivate connection, inspire belief, and uplift soulsâwill always be at their heart. Faith is timeless.Â

A Call to Action: Considering Technology and the Future of Spiritual Communities
================================================================================

[Heidi A. Campbell](https://intersections.ssrc.org/profiles/heidi-a-campbell/)

![Image 2](https://intersections.ssrc.org/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/andy-kelly-0E_vhMVqL9g-unsplash-scaled.jpg)

This forum explores themes emerging from the “Digital Technology and Online Spiritual Communities” workshop hosted by the SSRC in November 2024. The aim of this workshop—chaired by myself and Nabil Echchaibi of the University of Colorado Boulder —was to bring together leading scholars in Digital Religion Studies who are interested in the increasing reliance of spiritual communities on digital technologies. Digital religion is a growing area of research that examines how religion is practiced both online and offline and what occurs when religious groups connect these spaces through innovative spiritual practices. While the COVID-19 pandemic initially prompted many religious groups to use digital media for the first time, their continued investment in these technologies raises important questions about the long-term impact of digitally mediated spirituality on the future of religion.

The workshop focused on identifying common ways religious communities use digital media, their motivations for engaging with these technologies, and the ethical and spiritual implications of sustained use. Fourteen scholars shared insights from their research on the use of digital media among Christian, Jewish, Hindu, Muslim, and New Religious Movements. They described the emerging sphere of digital religion as a space of both community and contradiction—marked by flourishing and fear, misinformation and meaningful memories, and justice-seeking alongside discourse suppression.

Through a topic analysis of participant comments, aided by ChatGPT in identifying prominent themes, the workshop leaders distilled several recurring issues raised in discussion. This process led to the drafting of a “Call to Action and Reflection on Technology and the Future of Spiritual Communities,” which highlights major concerns about the nature and dominant traits of our emerging technoculture, particularly as they pertain to religious groups. This forum presents the call to action as a succinct summary of the workshop’s key discussions while also underscoring the ethical challenges within our technological ecologies that scholars and religious practitioners must closely examine.

The forum invites continued dialogue around this call to action with contributions from four workshop participants. Ruth Tsuria of Seton Hall University examines issues of religious authority, agency, and accountability in digital spaces. Margarita Guillory of Boston University investigates the complexities that arise for established communities when sacred rituals occur in technologically mediated settings. Elonda Clay of the Methodist Theological School in Ohio explores the impact of “one-size-fits-all” technologies on diverse religious institutions and communities. Finally, Gregory Price Grieve of the University of North Carolina Greensboro reflects on the possibilities and limitations of technological design in terms of historical and cultural religious practices. Together, these essays offer a starting point for deeper reflection on the nature of our technologies and the cultures they cultivate.

![Image 3](http://intersections.ssrc.org/wp-content/uploads/2025/04/jezael-melgoza-X7BSCrsdHVg-unsplash-1024x683.jpg)

Jezael Melgoza via Unsplash

### A Call to Action and Reflection on Technology and the Future of Spiritual Communities

As scholars and thinkers from multiple disciplines, we believe technology does—and will continue to—significantly impact spiritual communities, as well as their cultural perspectives, communal work, and communication practices. We offer this call to action as a starting point for critical reflection and future conversations on pressing issues facing spiritual communities in relation to our world’s current and emerging technological ecologies, particularly the new challenges created by artificial intelligence technologies and cultures.

**We affirm the following beliefs about the nature of technology:**

● **Technology Is a Tool:** It is neither inherently good nor bad but is profoundly shaped by human values, culture, and design. It mediates experiences, relationships, and ways of knowing.

● **Technology Shapes Human Experience:** It influences how we perceive ourselves, interact with others, and engage with our communities—including spiritual ones.

● **Technology Is Embedded in Power Structures:** It reflects and amplifies existing social, economic, and cultural dynamics, which must be critically examined.

● **Technology Is Transformative:** It creates opportunities for innovation, connection, and expression but also brings risks such as social disconnection, commodification, and the exploitation of humanity.

![Image 4](http://intersections.ssrc.org/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/chrisgoswami_A_hologram_of_a_preacher_giving_a_sermon_address_7f920dbc-edb4-48b3-a961-880ef37cde8b_1-1024x574-1.png)

Chris Goswami via Unsplash

**We identify the following areas where religion and technology intersect and call for deeper theoretical and critical reflection by scholars on the following issues:**

● **Community Formation and Transformation:** Technology, especially social media, facilitates the creation of new spiritual communities and enhances existing ones, yet also risks fragmenting traditional community structures and rituals.

● **Ritual and Representation:** Digital platforms provide spaces for shared religious practices and the dissemination of sacred knowledge but may also commodify, distort, or misappropriate these practices.

● **Authority and Agency:** Technology decentralizes authority, empowering individual expression but challenging institutional structures. This raises questions about the authenticity and legitimacy of spiritual guidance in digital spaces.

● **Sacred and Secular Intersections:** The blending of sacred and secular conceptual and ritual elements in technologically mediated spaces complicates the perceived and established relationship between traditional religious practices and digitized expressions of religion, which must be carefully considered.

![Image 5](http://intersections.ssrc.org/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/arif-riyanto-G1N9kDHqBrQ-unsplash-scaled-e1744660634516-981x1024.jpg)

Arif Riyanto via Unsplash

**Proposed Ethical Principles and Discussion Points for Evaluating Technology’s Impact on Spiritual Communities**

We advocate that the following principles guide ethical reflection and conversation on the growing role of technology in the work of spiritual communities and the increasing integration of AI into society more broadly:

1.   **Transparency:** Technology systems should be designed to operate in ways that offer clarity and insight into their processes, limitations, and goals, ensuring users understand their function and impact.
2.   **Complicating Institutionalized Legacies (of Equity and Inclusion):** Technologies should be designed to serve all people and must avoid reinforcing systemic biases or excluding marginalized voices.
3.   **Respect for Sacred Practices:** Technologies must honor the sacred aspects of religious practices and knowledge, avoiding commodification or trivialization.
4.   **Sustainability:** The design and use of technologies must account for environmental and social sustainability, ensuring that technology benefits future generations.
5.   **Accountability:** Developers, users, and institutions must share responsibility for the societal and ethical consequences of technology, ensuring it serves the common good.
6.   **Fostering Authenticity:** Digital and AI tools must prioritize genuine human connection and community, resisting trends toward superficial engagement or performative interactions.
7.   **Dialogue Between Tradition and Innovation:** Ongoing dialogue is needed to ensure that technological adoption respects and learns from historical and cultural religious practices rather than undermining them.
8.   **Ethical Labor Practices:** The often-invisible human labor behind technology (especially AI systems) must be recognized and fairly compensated, ensuring fair working conditions and respect for workers’ rights.

### **CALL TO ACTION**

We believe this call creates a space for fruitful conversations between scholars, spiritual leaders, technologists, and religious practitioners about where our future with technology is leading—especially for spiritual communities. This call is open to all individuals who wish to critically and collaboratively reflect on the social, cultural, and spiritual implications of religious communities’ engagement with current digital media, emerging technologies, and their potential long-term impacts. We recognize that this call does not address all the issues and complexities that emerging technologies create for spiritual communities. However, we believe these principles offer a valuable starting point for groups to consider how technologies might be used and designed to enhance individuals’ and communities’ ethical, social, and spiritual well-being.

*   [![Image 6: Profile picture of Heidi A. Campbell](https://intersections.ssrc.org/wp-content/uploads/2024/02/Heidi-Campbell.jpg)](https://intersections.ssrc.org/profiles/heidi-a-campbell/) ### [Heidi A. Campbell](https://intersections.ssrc.org/profiles/heidi-a-campbell/)  Professor & Presidential Impact Fellow, Texas A&M University    Affiliated Expert  HRLI Grantee

By analyzing data on attendance, engagement, and community needs, AI can help churches anticipate and plan for future growth. Incorporating AI into church work is about enhancing ministry and meeting the evolving needs of congregations. By taking these small steps, you can start to shift your thinking towards a future where AI is not just a technological tool, but a partner in deepening your church’s impact and reach.

FULL CONTENT:
![](https://www.facebook.com/tr?id=1245718778884681&ev=PageView&noscript=1)
![](https://exponential.org/wp-content/uploads/2023/10/Expo-Log_primary.png)
![](https://exponential.org/wp-content/uploads/2023/10/Expo-Logo_symbol-1.png)

# 9 AI Trends for Churches to Be Aware Of in 2024

#### February 5, 2024

#### By * [Kenny Jahng](https://exponential.org/person/kenny-jahng/)

As artificial intelligence (AI) technology continues to advance, the role of AI in ministry is becoming increasingly vital. 

Pastors seeking to integrate AI into their churches need to be aware of the latest trends to effectively utilize this technology. I want to share nine key AI trends I see coming in this year, each with specific examples and expanded insights, to help you envision the future of AI in church work.

## **9 Trends In AI For Churches**

### **1. Customized Sermon and Teaching Aids**

AI tools are now crucial in crafting sermons and teachings that resonate deeply with diverse congregations. These tools analyze various data points, like community interests and feedback, to help pastors tailor their messages more effectively. As AI becomes more sophisticated, it can provide nuanced insights into biblical texts and contemporary issues, making sermons more relevant and impactful.

### **2. Enhanced Community Engagement Platforms**

AI-driven platforms are revolutionizing how churches engage with their members. From personalized spiritual growth plans to virtual prayer groups, these platforms offer tailored experiences that cater to individual needs. AI facilitates interactive and dynamic faith-based learning, making religious education more accessible and engaging for people of all ages.

### **3. Digital Missionary Tools**

In an increasingly digital world, AI is a powerful tool for expanding the church’s reach. Digital missionary tools such as social media outreach and AI-driven evangelism chatbots are making it easier to connect with people across the globe. These tools provide a platform for spiritual counseling and evangelism, offering new avenues for spreading the gospel.

### **4. Virtual Reality (VR) Spiritual Experiences**

Virtual Reality (VR) is opening up new possibilities for spiritual experiences. Churches are using VR to create immersive tours of religious sites and interactive biblical stories. This technology is especially appealing to younger generations, providing a novel way to engage with faith and scripture in a digital age.

### **5. Advanced Language Translation for Inclusivity**

The ability to offer services in multiple languages is crucial for modern churches. AI-powered translation services are making this more achievable, allowing churches to cater to a diverse congregation. This trend is key to fostering an inclusive environment where everyone, regardless of their native language, feels welcomed and understood.

### **6. Predictive Analytics for Church Growth and Health**

Predictive analytics are becoming an essential tool for church leadership. By analyzing data on attendance, engagement, and community needs, AI can help churches anticipate and plan for future growth. This technology aids in making informed decisions about resource allocation, program development, and pastoral care.

### **7. AI-Enhanced Music and Worship Experiences**

Music is a central part of worship, and AI is helping to enhance this experience. Churches are using AI to suggest songs and arrangements that align with sermon themes and congregational preferences. This technology also aids in composing original music, ensuring every worship experience is unique and meaningful.

### **8. AI in Biblical Research and Study**

AI is transforming biblical research and study. By analyzing historical texts and linguistic patterns, AI provides deeper insights into the scriptures. This technology is an invaluable resource for pastors and scholars, helping them uncover new understandings and interpretations of biblical narratives.

### **9. Mental Health and Wellness Support**

Mental health is a growing concern, and churches are turning to AI for support. AI tools offer initial assessments and direct individuals to appropriate resources. These systems can also identify those in need of urgent pastoral care, ensuring timely and effective support for mental wellness.

Incorporating AI into church work is about enhancing ministry and meeting the evolving needs of congregations. By understanding these trends, pastors can effectively use AI to support and grow their ministries in 2024 and beyond.

### **FINAL THOUGHTS: Taking Your Next Steps in AI Integration for Church Work**

As you contemplate integrating AI into your church’s activities, here are five practical steps to consider. These suggestions are designed to help you take advantage of the potential AI offers, in line with the trends we’ve discussed:

By taking these small steps, you can start to shift your thinking towards a future where AI is not just a technological tool, but a partner in deepening your church’s impact and reach.   
  
Embrace the possibilities with optimism and a willingness to learn and adapt, and you’ll find AI can be a powerful ally in your ministry.

***What is the first obstacle in your mind to exploring AI for church ministry work?***

enriched_points.point_title: Increased Integration of Mental Health Support
enriched_points.expanded_content: Digital faith communities will increasingly incorporate mental health resources, using AI and online platforms to provide accessible support, fostering holistic well-being alongside spiritual growth.
As technology advances, digital worship practices will evolve, incorporating VR, AR, and interactive elements, enhancing engagement and creating immersive spiritual experiences for participants.

enriched_points.point_title: Focus on Community Building and Inclusivity
enriched_points.expanded_content: Future digital faith communities will prioritize inclusivity, fostering diverse connections through social media and online forums, ensuring all voices are heard and valued in the spiritual journey.
